what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment id to an image asset URI and noting when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
Added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to design-assets/icons/Feature_6_DayNight
conventions: Channel-memory attachment stored as a markdown file
Asset referenced by a URI target
concerns: No body or descriptive content beyond metadata
Target URI cannot be resolved from this file alone
No author, source, or provenance information
No status, version, or size fields
notes: A minimal ledger entry that registers an image attachment for a design-assets channel icon.
questions: What visual content does Feature_6_DayNight represent?
Where is the actual image file stored?
Who added this attachment and in what context?
How is the image:// URI resolved by the channel-memory system?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is an attachment record that points to an image asset and records its addition time.
intention_signals: To track image attachments within a channel memory system so each attachment has a stable id, a target asset, and a creation timestamp.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme to target a channel-specific design asset.
contribution: Provides a stable attachment identifier and a pointer to a specific design-assets image, which a fused system can use to correlate attachments with their target assets and addition timestamps.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-d2d25119 in the design-assets channel pointing to the image asset image://design-assets/icons/Feature_6_DayNight and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced by id and tracked by creation time.
